div.h1: Start Times Hash Map Keying Strategy
div.h2: Memory Safety and Race Condition Analysis for eBPF Latency Tracers

g.text: ❌ SCENARIO A: Keying by TGID (Process ID)
✅ SCENARIO B: Keying by PID (Thread ID)

div.p: To avoid the race condition, extract the lower 32 bits of the bpf_get_current_pid_tgid() return value.
// 2. Correct extraction (Userspace TID is lower 32 bits) u32 tid = (u32)pid_tgid;
// 3. Store with Thread-Level Isolation bpf_map_update_elem(&start_times, &tid, &ts, BPF_ANY);

Why TGID Fails: In Linux, all threads in a process share the same tgid. Using tgid as a key creates a Shared Resource Race Condition where concurrent syscall entries from different threads overwrite each other's state.
